concerns.text: The plan cites 'MemLens' via HuggingFace URLs but proposes calculating 'Object Detection Recall against ground-truth bounding boxes' (research.md: Model & Methodology) and using them for Fine store validation (FR-009). The MemLens dataset is a multimodal long-term memory benchmark focused on QA pairs and session context; it does not natively provide per-image ground-truth bounding boxes required to compute Object Detection Recall. Without an external, cited source for these bounding boxes (e.g., a specific COCO subset or a separate annotation file linked to MemLens), the 'Fine' store validation metric is impossible to compute, rendering the statistical comparison of visual fidelity invalid.

The plan assumes the MemLens dataset contains 'images (or image paths)' sufficient for running YOLOv8-Tiny to generate object-level captions. However, the cited MemLens sources (JSON/Parquet) are primarily text-heavy QA benchmarks. If the images are not directly embedded or linked in a format accessible to the CPU pipeline without massive download overhead, or if the dataset relies on external image hosting that is rate-limited/unreliable, the 'Fine' store construction (FR-002) will fail. The plan must explicitly cite the specific image source/URL structure within MemLens to confirm image accessibility for the CPU pipeline.
